sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: τεισώμεθʼ αὐτόν . πρῶτα δʼ ἔκστησον φρενῶν ,
sections.translation: Let us punish him . First drive him out of his wits , send upon him a dizzying madness , since if he is of sound mind he will not consent to wear women ’ s clothing , but driven out of his senses he will put it on . I want him to be a source of laughter to the Thebans , led through the city in

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: γυναικόμορφον ἀγόμενον διʼ ἄστεως
sections.translation: women ’ s guise after making such terrible threats in the past . But now I will go to fit on Pentheus the dress he will wear to the house of Hades , slaughtered by his mother ’ s hands . He will recognize the son of Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: Διόνυσον , ὃς πέφυκεν ἐν τέλει θεός ,
sections.translation: Dionysus , who is in fact a god , the most terrible and yet most mild to men .

sections.label: Section 1.862
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἐν παννυχίοις χοροῖς
sections.translation: Shall I move my white foot in the night-long dance , aroused to a frenzy ,

sections.label: Section 1.865
sections.original: εἰς αἰθέρα δροσερὸν ῥίπτουσʼ ,
sections.translation: throwing my head to the dewy air , like a fawn sporting in the green pleasures of the meadow , when it has escaped a fearful chase beyond the watchers

sections.label: Section 1.870
sections.original: εὐπλέκτων ὑπὲρ ἀρκύων ,
sections.translation: over the well-woven nets , and the hunter hastens his dogs on their course with his call , while she , with great exertion and a storm-swift running , rushes along the plain by the river , rejoicing

sections.label: Section 1.875
sections.original: βροτῶν ἐρημίαις σκιαρο-
sections.translation: in the solitude apart from men and in the thickets of the shady-foliaged woods . What is wisdom ? Or what greater honor do the gods give to mortals than to hold one ’ s hand

sections.label: Section 1.880
sections.original: τῶν ἐχθρῶν κρείσσω κατέχειν ;
sections.translation: in strength over the head of enemies ? What is good is always dear .

sections.label: Section 1.882
sections.original: ὁρμᾶται μόλις , ἀλλʼ ὅμως
sections.translation: Divine strength is roused with difficulty , but still is sure . It chastises those mortals

sections.label: Section 1.885
sections.original: τούς τʼ ἀγνωμοσύναν τιμῶν-
sections.translation: who honor folly and those who in their insanity do not extol the gods . The gods cunningly conceal the long pace of time and

sections.label: Section 1.890
sections.original: θηρῶσιν τὸν ἄσεπτον . οὐ
sections.translation: hunt the impious . For it is not right to determine or plan anything beyond the laws . For it is a light expense to hold that whatever is divine has power ,

sections.label: Section 1.895
sections.original: τό τʼ ἐν χρόνῳ μακρῷ νόμιμον
sections.translation: and that which has been law for a long time is eternal and has its origin in nature . What is wisdom ? Or what greater honor do the gods give to mortals than to hold one ’ s hand

sections.label: Section 1.900
sections.original: τῶν ἐχθρῶν κρείσσω κατέχειν ;
sections.translation: in strength over the head of enemies ? What is good is always dear .

sections.label: Section 902
sections.original: εὐδαίμων μὲν ὃς ἐκ θαλάσσας
sections.translation: Happy is he [*] who has fled a storm on the sea , and reached harbor . Happy too is he who has overcome his hardships .

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: ἐγένεθʼ · ἑτέρᾳ δʼ ἕτερος ἕτερον
sections.translation: One surpass another in different ways , in wealth or power . There are innumerable hopes to innumerable men , and some result in wealth to mortals , while others fail .

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: τὸ δὲ κατʼ ἦμαρ ὅτῳ βίοτος
sections.translation: But I call him blessed whose life is happy day to day .

sections.label: Section 912
sections.original: σὲ τὸν πρόθυμον ὄνθʼ ἃ μὴ χρεὼν ὁρᾶν
sections.translation: You who are eager to see what you ought not and hasty in pursuit of what ought not to be pursued — I mean you , Pentheus , come forth before the house , be seen by me ,

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: σκευὴν γυναικὸς μαινάδος βάκχης ἔχων ,
sections.translation: wearing the clothing of a woman , of an inspired maenad , a spy upon your mother and her company . Pentheus emerges . In appearance you are like one of Kadmos ’ daughters .

sections.label: Section 918
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὁρᾶν μοι δύο μὲν ἡλίους δοκῶ ,
sections.translation: Oh look ! I think I see two suns , and twin Thebes , the seven-gated city .

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: καὶ ταῦρος ἡμῖν πρόσθεν ἡγεῖσθαι δοκεῖς
sections.translation: And you seem to lead me , being like a bull and horns seem to grow on your head . But were you ever before a beast ? For you have certainly now become a bull .

sections.label: Section 923
sections.original: ὁ θεὸς ὁμαρτεῖ , πρόσθεν ὢν οὐκ εὐμενής ,
sections.translation: The god accompanies us , now at truce with us , though formerly not propitious . Now you see what you should see .

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: τί φαίνομαι δῆτʼ ; οὐχὶ τὴν Ἰνοῦς στάσιν
sections.translation: How do I look ? Don ’ t I have the posture of Ino , or of my mother Agave ?

sections.label: Section 927
sections.original: αὐτὰς ἐκείνας εἰσορᾶν δοκῶ σʼ ὁρῶν .
sections.translation: Looking at you I think I see them . But this lock of your hair has come out of place , not the way I arranged it under your headband .

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: ἔνδον προσείων αὐτὸν ἀνασείων τʼ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: I displaced it indoors , shaking my head forwards and backwards and practising my Bacchic revelry .

sections.label: Section 932
sections.original: ἀλλʼ αὐτὸν ἡμεῖς , οἷς σε θεραπεύειν μέλει ,
sections.translation: But I who ought to wait on you will re-arrange it . Hold up your head .

sections.label: Section 933
sections.original: πάλιν καταστελοῦμεν · ἀλλʼ ὄρθου κάρα .
sections.translation: Here , you arrange it ; for I depend on you , indeed .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ζῶναί τέ σοι χαλῶσι κοὐχ ἑξῆς πέπλων
sections.translation: Your girdle has come loose , and the pleats of your gown do not extend regularly down around your ankles .

sections.label: Section 937
sections.original: κἀμοὶ δοκοῦσι παρά γε δεξιὸν πόδα ·
sections.translation: At least on my right leg , I believe they don ’ t . But on this side the robe sits well around the back of my leg .

sections.label: Section 939
sections.original: ἦ πού με τῶν σῶν πρῶτον ἡγήσῃ φίλων ,
sections.translation: You will surely consider me the best of your friends ,

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: ὅταν παρὰ λόγον σώφρονας βάκχας ἴδῃς .
sections.translation: when contrary to your expectation you see the Bacchae acting modestly .

sections.label: Section 941
sections.original: πότερα δὲ θύρσον δεξιᾷ λαβὼν χερὶ
sections.translation: But shall I be more like a maenad holding the thyrsos in my right hand , or in my left ?

sections.label: Section 943
sections.original: ἐν δεξιᾷ χρὴ χἅμα δεξιῷ ποδὶ
sections.translation: You must hold it in your right hand and raise your right foot in unison with it . I praise you for having changed your mind .

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἂν δυναίμην τὰς Κιθαιρῶνος πτυχὰς
sections.translation: Could I carry on my shoulders the glens of Kithairon , Bacchae and all ?

sections.label: Section 947
sections.original: δύναιʼ ἄν , εἰ βούλοιο · τὰς δὲ πρὶν φρένας
sections.translation: You could if you were willing . The state of mind you had before was unsound , but now you think as you ought .

sections.label: Section 949
sections.original: μοχλοὺς φέρωμεν ; ἢ χεροῖν ἀνασπάσω
sections.translation: Shall we bring levers ? Or shall I draw them up with my hands ,

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: κορυφαῖς ὑποβαλὼν ὦμον ἢ βραχίονα ;
sections.translation: putting a shoulder or arm under the mountain-tops ?

sections.label: Section 951
sections.original: μὴ σύ γε τὰ Νυμφῶν διολέσῃς ἱδρύματα
sections.translation: But don ’ t destroy the seats of the Nymphs and the places where Pan plays his pipes .

sections.label: Section 953
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας · οὐ σθένει νικητέον
sections.translation: Well said . The women are not to be taken by force ; I will hide in the pines .

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: κρύψῃ σὺ κρύψιν ἥν σε κρυφθῆναι χρεών ,
sections.translation: You will hide yourself as you should be hidden , coming as a crafty spy on the Maenads .

sections.label: Section 957
sections.original: καὶ μὴν δοκῶ σφᾶς ἐν λόχμαις ὄρνιθας ὣς
sections.translation: Oh , yes ! I imagine that like birds they are in the bushes held in the sweetest grips of love .

sections.label: Section 959
sections.original: οὐκοῦν ἐπʼ αὐτὸ τοῦτʼ ἀποστέλλῃ φύλαξ ·
sections.translation: You have been sent as a guard against this very event .

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: λήψῃ δʼ ἴσως σφᾶς , ἢν σὺ μὴ ληφθῇς πάρος .
sections.translation: Perhaps you will catch them , if you yourself are not caught before .

sections.label: Section 961
sections.original: κόμιζε διὰ μέσης με Θηβαίας χθονός ·
sections.translation: Bring me through the midst of the Theban land . I am the only man of them who dares to perform this deed .

sections.label: Section 963
sections.original: μόνος σὺ πόλεως τῆσδʼ ὑπερκάμνεις , μόνος ·
sections.translation: You alone bear the burden for this city , you alone . Therefore the labors which are proper await you .

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: ἕπου δέ · πομπὸς δʼ εἶμʼ ἐγὼ σωτήριος ,
sections.translation: Follow me . I am your saving guide : another will lead you down from there .

sections.label: Section 966b
sections.original: ἡ τεκοῦσά γε .
sections.translation: Yes , my mother .

sections.label: Section 967
sections.original: ἐπίσημον ὄντα πᾶσιν .
sections.translation: And you will be remarkable to all .

sections.label: Section 967b
sections.original: ἐπὶ τόδʼ ἔρχομαι .
sections.translation: I am going for this reason .

sections.label: Section 968
sections.original: φερόμενος ἥξεις ⋯
sections.translation: You will return here being carried —

sections.label: Section 968b
sections.original: ἁβρότητʼ ἐμὴν λέγεις .
sections.translation: You talk of a delicacy for me .

sections.label: Section 969
sections.original: ἐν χερσὶ μητρός .
sections.translation: In the arms of your mother .

sections.label: Section 969b
sections.original: καὶ τρυφᾶν μʼ ἀναγκάσεις .
sections.translation: You will force me to luxury .

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: τρυφάς γε τοιάσδε .
sections.translation: Yes indeed , such luxury !

sections.label: Section 970b
sections.original: ἀξίων μὲν ἅπτομαι .
sections.translation: I will get what I deserve .

sections.label: Section 971
sections.original: δεινὸς σὺ δεινὸς κἀπὶ δείνʼ ἔρχῃ πάθη ,
sections.translation: You are terrible , terrible , and you go to terrible sufferings , so that you will find a renown reaching to heaven . Reach out your hands , Agave , and you too , her sisters , daughters of Kadmos . I lead this young man

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: τόνδʼ εἰς ἀγῶνα μέγαν , ὁ νικήσων δʼ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: to a great contest , and Bromius and I will be the victors . The rest the matter itself will show .

sections.label: Section 1.977
sections.original: ἴτε θοαὶ Λύσσας κύνες ἴτʼ εἰς ὄρος ,
sections.translation: Go to the mountain , go , fleet hounds of Madness , where the daughters of Kadmos hold their company , and drive them raving

sections.label: Section 1.980
sections.original: ἐπὶ τὸν ἐν γυναικομίμῳ στολᾷ
sections.translation: against the mad spy on the Maenads , the one dressed in women ’ s attire . His mother will be the first to see him from a smooth rock or crag , as he lies in ambush , and she will cry out to the maenads :

sections.label: Section 1.985
sections.original: Τίς ὅδʼ ὀρειδρόμων
sections.translation: Who is this seeker of the mountain-going Kadmeans who has come to the mountain , to the mountain , Bacchae ? Who bore him ? For he was not born from a woman ’ s blood , but is the offspring of some lioness

sections.label: Section 1.990
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἢ Γοργόνων Λιβυσσᾶν γένος .
sections.translation: or of Libyan Gorgons . Let manifest justice go forth , let it go with sword in hand , slaying through the throat
